Call a Shot.test: None; the associated attack is resolved normally.
requirements: Must be combined with a Fire Weapon, Throw Weapon, or Melee Attack action.
Call a Shot.attack_restriction: Modifies an attack rather than being a separate attack.
Call a Shot.description: The character aims for a vulnerable or specific part of the target. The selected called-shot effect and its modifier are applied to the attack made during the same Action Phase.

requirements: The character must be connected to the device through a wired or wireless direct neural interface.
Change Linked Device Mode.description:
Activate, deactivate, or change the operating mode of a directly linked device. Examples include activating cyberware, changing a smartgun's firing mode, changing a smartlinked shotgun's choke, switching vision modes, hiding a commlink, or disabling wireless functionality.

Some devices require more time where their individual rules say so.

requirements: The object must be held by the character.
Drop Object.description: Drop one held object, or one object from each hand, as a single Free Action. Fragile items or objects dropped into a hostile environment may be damaged at the gamemaster's discretion.

requirements: The character cannot be surprised.
Drop Prone.description: Kneel or immediately drop into the prone position. A surprised character cannot use this action.

requirements: The character must be linked to a ready smartgun fitted with the necessary system.
Eject Smartgun Clip.description: Use a mental command to eject the smartgun's current clip. Inserting a replacement clip still requires a separate Simple Action.

Gesture.test: Intuition (2) for an observer unfamiliar with the gestures.
requirements: The message must be brief enough to communicate through a few quick gestures.
Gesture.description: Communicate a short instruction or message using quick gestures. Someone unfamiliar with the gesture system may need to interpret it with an Intuition Test.

Multiple Attacks.test: The associated attack dice pool is split between the chosen attacks or targets.
requirements: Must be combined with an eligible Fire Weapon, Throw Weapon, Melee Attack, Reckless Spellcasting, or Cast Spell action.
Multiple Attacks.attack_restriction: Modifies an attack rather than being a separate attack.
Multiple Attacks.description: Attack more than one target, or make eligible attacks with more than one weapon, during a single attack action. The original dice pool is divided among the separate attacks, and all relevant off-hand and situational modifiers still apply.

Run.test: None for ordinary running; Sprinting requires a separate Complex Action and Running Test.
requirements: The character's total movement during the Combat Turn must exceed their Walking rate.
Run.attack_restriction: Not an attack, but running applies movement modifiers.
Run.description: Declare movement beyond the character's Walking rate for the Combat Turn. The character is considered running and receives the normal running modifiers until the movement state changes under the movement rules.

requirements: Limited to one short phrase, sentence, or DNI text message per Free Action.
Speak/Text/Transmit Phrase.description: Speak, transmit, or send one short phrase. Longer conversations require additional Free Actions and must remain plausible within a three-second Combat Turn.

requirements: The character must be carrying the focus and able to use it.
Activate Focus.description: Activate a carried magical focus so that its powers become available under the focus rules.

requirements: The spirit must already have been summoned and placed on standby.
Call Spirit.description: Call a spirit that the summoner previously placed on standby, bringing it back into active service.

requirements: The character must be able to operate the device through its controls, a commlink, or another suitable wired or wireless connection.
Change Device Mode.description:
Activate, deactivate, or change a device's mode using a switch, virtual control, or remote command. This can include weapon modes, shotgun chokes, vision systems, commlink status, or wireless functionality.

Equipment with more involved controls may require a different action specified by its own rules.

requirements: The character must be holding a ready firearm.
Change Gun Mode.description: Change the firing mode of a ready firearm or alter a shotgun's choke. A properly linked smartgun reduces this to the Change Linked Device Mode Free Action.

requirements: The spirit or group of spirits must be under the summoner's control.
Command Spirit.description: Issue one command to a controlled spirit or to a group of controlled spirits.

requirements: The spirit must be under the summoner's control.
Dismiss Spirit.description: Release a spirit from the summoner's control. The spirit is not automatically returned to its home metaplane and may act as it chooses.

Fire Bow.test: Archery + Agility [Accuracy], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The bow must be ready and already loaded with an arrow.
Fire Bow.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action for the Action Phase.
Fire Bow.description: Fire one arrow from a loaded bow. Nocking the arrow beforehand uses the Reload Weapon Simple Action.

Fire Weapon (SA, SS, BF or FA).test: Appropriate firearm skill + Agility [Accuracy], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The firearm must be ready and capable of the selected firing mode.
Fire Weapon (SA, SS, BF or FA).attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action for the Action Phase; no other attack action may be taken that phase.
Fire Weapon (SA, SS, BF or FA).description:
Fire a ready firearm using Semi-Automatic, Single-Shot, Burst-Fire, or the Simple-Action form of Full-Auto.

  • Burst Fire: expends 3 rounds.
  • Full-Auto: expends 6 rounds when used as a Simple Action.
  • Two eligible weapons may be fired by combining this with Multiple Attacks, splitting the dice pool and applying off-hand modifiers.

requirements: The firearm must be ready.
The previous removable clip must already have been removed.
A fresh clip must be available.
Insert Clip.description: Insert a fresh removable clip into a ready firearm. Removing the old clip and inserting the new one are separate Simple Actions unless another rule changes the process.

Observe in Detail.test: Perception + Intuition [Mental], with modifiers appropriate to the sense and situation.
Observe in Detail.description: Closely examine a person, object, or part of the environment. Obvious information is normally noticed without this action; Observe in Detail is used when the character needs a Perception Test to identify finer details.

Pick Up/Put Down Object.test: None unless unusual circumstances require one.
requirements: The object must be within reach.
Pick Up/Put Down Object.description: Carefully pick up an object or place down an object being held. Unlike the Drop Object Free Action, this action assumes enough care to reduce the risk of damaging the item.

Quick Draw.test: Appropriate Weapon Skill + Reaction [Physical] (3), or threshold 2 with a quick-draw holster.
requirements: The pistol, pistol-sized weapon, blade, or small throwing weapon must be properly holstered, sheathed, or carried on a suitable sling.
Quick Draw.attack_restriction: Drawing and immediately attacking counts as the character's attack action for the phase.
Quick Draw.description:
Attempt to draw a suitable weapon and attack with it as one Simple Action.

  • Success: draw and attack immediately.
  • Failure: the weapon is drawn but cannot be used with that action.
  • Glitch: the weapon becomes stuck or is dropped and the character loses their remaining actions.
  • Critical glitch: the weapon may be thrown out of reach, discharged in the holster, or suffer another serious mishap.

Two weapons may be quick-drawn together by splitting the test pool and applying off-hand penalties.

requirements: The weapon must be accessible.
Ready Weapon.description:
Draw, pick up, unsling, or otherwise prepare a firearm, melee weapon, throwing weapon, bow, mounted weapon, or vehicle weapon for use.

Improperly stored or awkward weapons may require a Complex Action at the gamemaster's discretion. One action can ready a number of small throwing weapons equal to half the character's Agility, rounded up.

Reckless Spellcasting.test: Resolved using the normal Spellcasting Test for the selected spell.
requirements: The character must be capable of casting the selected spell.
Reckless Spellcasting.attack_restriction: Counts as an attack action when the spell is an attack; otherwise it still uses one of the phase's Simple Actions.
Reckless Spellcasting.description: Cast a spell more quickly as a Simple Action. Reckless casting increases the spell's Drain Value under the spellcasting rules.

requirements: The weapon and ammunition must be accessible.
Only reload methods listed as Simple may use this action.
Reload Weapon.description: Perform a reload method that takes a Simple Action. Under the Core Rulebook table, this includes removing or inserting a removable clip and nocking one arrow.

requirements: The firearm must be ready and currently contain a removable clip.
Remove Clip.description: Remove the current clip from a ready firearm. Retrieving and inserting a replacement clip requires another Simple Action.

requirements: The character must be capable of astral perception.
Shift Perception.description: Shift perception from the physical plane to astral perception, or return from astral perception to ordinary physical perception.

Stand Up.test: Body + Willpower (2) if wounded; otherwise no test.
requirements: The character must currently be prone or lying down.
Stand Up.description: Rise from the prone position. A wounded character must pass the listed test, including wound modifiers, to stand successfully.

requirements: The firearm, bow, or throwing weapon must be ready.
Take Aim.description:
Prepare a more accurate attack with a ready ranged weapon. Each consecutive Take Aim action provides either +1 die or +1 Accuracy to the eventual attack.

  • The maximum accumulated bonus is half the character's Willpower, rounded up.
  • The benefit is lost if the character takes any other action, including a Free Action, before attacking.
  • The first Take Aim used with a targeting scope or image magnification enables the device but grants no additional aiming bonus.

requirements: Suitable cover must be available.
The character cannot be surprised.
Take Cover.description: Deliberately position the character behind available cover to gain the applicable cover bonus on Defense Tests.

Throw Weapon.test: Throwing Weapons + Agility [Physical], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The throwing weapon must already be ready.
Throw Weapon.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action for the Action Phase; no other attack action may be taken that phase.
Throw Weapon.description: Throw one ready weapon. Multiple ready throwing weapons may be used against eligible targets within Short or Medium range by combining this action with Multiple Attacks and splitting the dice pool.

Use Simple Device.test: Normally none; the device may require a test under its own rules.
requirements: The device must be usable through a simple control such as a trigger, single key, switch, or icon.
Use Simple Device.description: Operate a straightforward device whose function is activated through one uncomplicated movement or control input.

requirements: The character must be capable of astral projection.
Astral Projection.description: Leave the physical body and project the character's consciousness onto the astral plane.

Banish Spirit.test: Banishing + Magic [Astral], opposed according to the Banishing rules.
requirements: A valid spirit target must be available.
Banish Spirit.attack_restriction: Not treated as an ordinary physical attack.
Banish Spirit.description: Enter a Banishing contest to weaken or force a spirit away from the physical plane.

Cast Spell.test: Spellcasting + Magic [Force], opposed or resisted according to the selected spell.
requirements: The character must know and be capable of casting the selected spell.
Cast Spell.attack_restriction: Counts as an attack action when the selected spell is an attack.
Cast Spell.description: Cast one spell using the standard spellcasting process, including selecting Force, resolving the spell, and resisting Drain.

Fire Full-Auto Weapon.test: Appropriate firearm skill + Agility [Accuracy], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The firearm must be ready and capable of Full-Auto fire.
At least 10 rounds must be available.
Fire Full-Auto Weapon.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action.
Fire Full-Auto Weapon.description: Fire a ready weapon using the Complex-Action Full-Auto attack, expending 10 rounds. Multiple eligible weapons may be used with Multiple Attacks, with the dice pool split and off-hand modifiers applied.

Fire Long Burst or Semi-Auto Burst.test: Appropriate firearm skill + Agility [Accuracy], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The firearm must be ready and capable of the selected firing mode.
Fire Long Burst or Semi-Auto Burst.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action.
Fire Long Burst or Semi-Auto Burst.description: Fire a ready firearm as a Long Burst or Semi-Auto Burst. Eligible attacks can be divided between multiple targets at Short or Medium range by combining the action with Multiple Attacks.

Fire Mounted or Vehicle Weapon.test: Resolved with the appropriate Gunnery or weapon test under the vehicle-combat rules.
requirements: The mounted or vehicle weapon must already be ready and controllable by the character.
Fire Mounted or Vehicle Weapon.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action.
Fire Mounted or Vehicle Weapon.description: Fire a previously readied weapon mounted on a vehicle, drone, emplacement, or similar platform.

Load and Fire Bow.test: Archery + Agility [Accuracy], opposed by the target's Defense Test.
requirements: The bow must already be ready.
A suitable arrow must be accessible.
Load and Fire Bow.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action.
Load and Fire Bow.description: Nock an arrow and fire the bow as a single Complex Action. The order may be reversed when appropriate: fire a loaded bow and then nock the next arrow.

Melee Attack.test: Appropriate melee skill + Agility [Accuracy or Physical], opposed by the target's melee Defense Test.
requirements: The target must be within the attacker's melee reach.
Melee Attack.attack_restriction: Counts as the character's attack action.
Melee Attack.description: Make one armed or unarmed close-combat attack. Multiple targets within melee range may be attacked by adding Multiple Attacks and splitting the dice pool.

requirements: The weapon and ammunition must be accessible.
The selected reload method must require a Complex Action.
Reload Weapon.description: Reload a weapon using a method that requires a Complex Action, including speed loaders, break-action weapons, belts, drums, internal magazines, cylinders, muzzle-loaders, or manually filling clips and ammunition feeds.

requirements: The character must have a vehicle control rig.
The vehicle or drone must be rigger-adapted and accessible through a valid connection.
Rigger Jump In.description: Transfer the rigger's control perspective into a compatible vehicle or drone and begin controlling it through jumped-in virtual reality.

requirements: The character must be running and able to move.
Sprint.description: Attempt to increase the character's running distance for the Combat Turn. Each hit adds distance according to the character's metatype sprint-increase value.

Summoning.test: Summoning + Magic [Force], opposed by the spirit's Force.
requirements: The character must be able to summon the selected spirit type.
Summoning.description: Attempt to summon a spirit and gain services from it under the Summoning rules.

Use Skill.test: The test associated with the selected skill and task.
requirements: The task must be suitable for completion or meaningful progress through a Complex Action.
Use Skill.attack_restriction: Depends on the selected skill and task.
Use Skill.description: Perform a skilled task that demands substantial concentration, coordination, or effort and is not covered by a more specific listed action.

Removable Clip.result: Remove or insert one removable clip.

Speed Loader.result: Use a speed loader to completely load the firearm.

Fill Clip.result: Insert a number of rounds equal to Agility into a clip.

Belt Fed.result: Remove or insert an ammunition belt.

Fill Belt/Drum.result: Insert a number of rounds equal to Agility into a belt or drum.

Internal Magazine.result: Insert a number of rounds equal to Agility into the internal magazine.

Cylinder.result: Insert a number of rounds equal to Agility into the cylinder.

action_rules.Free Actions:
Free Actions
Normally one Free Action is available during each Initiative Pass. It may be used during the character's Action Phase or later in the pass. Before the character's first Action Phase, a Free Action may only be taken if the character is not surprised. Free Actions normally require no test.
action_rules.Simple Actions:
Simple Actions
A character may normally take two Simple Actions during an Action Phase, plus one Free Action. Only one of the two Simple Actions may be an attack action.
action_rules.Complex Actions:
Complex Actions
A character may normally take one Complex Action during an Action Phase, plus one Free Action.
action_rules.Unlisted Actions:
Unlisted Actions
The gamemaster assigns an unlisted activity as Free, Simple, or Complex by comparing its effort, concentration, and finesse to the listed actions.
action_rules.Scope:
Scope
This file contains the generic combat Free, Simple, and Complex Actions from the Core Rulebook. Matrix-specific actions and Interrupt Actions are separate systems and are not included.
